title: GPSR: null island check bypass with epsilon coordinates
description:
File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:782-785

The null island rejection uses exact floating-point equality:

```python
if lat == 0.0 and lon == 0.0:
    logger.warning("gpsr: rejecting null island coords (0, 0)")
    return None
```

WHY IT'S A BUG: Malformed GPS data often produces values near (0, 0) but not exactly equal. Values like (0.0000001, 0.0) or (1e-10, 1e-10) will bypass this check. Null island (0, 0) is the default for uninitialized or invalid GPS data, and nearby values should also be rejected.

IMPACT: GPSR routing could use effectively-invalid coordinates from sensors that produce near-zero rather than exact-zero garbage.

FIX: Use epsilon comparison, e.g.:
```python
NULL_ISLAND_EPSILON = 0.001  # ~111 meters
if abs(lat) < NULL_ISLAND_EPSILON and abs(lon) < NULL_ISLAND_EPSILON:
    ...
```
